calendar.matters:
ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Evidentiary Hearing On The Petition For Instructions To Trustee
Off Calendar Oct-21-2016 Off Calendar

Petition For Instructions To Trustee And For Order Modifying Order Dated August 26,2010
Off Calendar Oct-26-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Thomas Klitgaard for Boy Scouts of America. Andrew Zabronsky for (trustee) Wells Fargo Bank. Stephanie Roeser for the San Francisco Foundation with Sindy Craig also present.
Ken Melbourne, John Riggenberg & Don Levine also present with Mr. Klitgaard.

Mr. Klitgaard gave a history of the case, that they mediated with Judge Wiss and reached a settlement agreement, that everyone signed the agreement and a stipulation was filed, that they are here today to ask the Court to approve the settlement agreement, that he and Mr. Zabronsky have exchanged proposed orders, that there are 2 disputes, one is whether the entire settlement agreement should be adopted by the Court, that Wells Fargo does not agree with this, that Wells Fargo wants the current petition dismissed with prejudice, that he is reluctant to do so, cited 664.6 regarding conditions, that the Attorney General has not signed the settlement agreement but has no objections. The Court asked if counsel had received input on the current order. Mr. Klitgaard reported that he emailed it to both counsel yesterday and spoke with Mr. Zabronsky this morning regarding it. The Court asked Mr. Zabronsky for his thoughts on the revised order. Mr. Zabronsky responded that he has not looked at the details, that he understands it contains the same things that have been objected to, that they stipulated that the petition would be granted in part, that the other claims would be dismissed, that the Boy Scouts are claiming the right to keep the petition on calendar and are not releasing the claims, that it is a repudiation of the settlement agreement, that they cannot come in to Court and aske for confirmation of a settlement agreement that is not before the Court, that the claims need to either be denied or dismissed. Ms. Roeser stated that she agrees with Wells Fargo, that they are concerned that if the Boy Scouts order goes through there will be future litigation. Mr. Klitgaard stated that the Scouts agreed to mediation with Judge Wiss, that Wells Fargo did not file a response to the petition until right before mediation and that he would like the courtroom cleared to discuss the settlement agreement. The Court stated that it will not be necessary for what is being decided today and that the only argument is as to why your order is superior. Mr. Klitgaard stated that the order incorporates what was agreed to in the settlement agreement, that he asks why they are requiring a dismissal and that Wells Fargo has never provided an accounting. Mr. Zabronsky stated that this is the first time he is hearing of a request for an accounting, that the accounting is not due until this year is over and that it has nothing to do with what we are here for today. The Court took the Petition for Instructions to Trustee and for Order Modifying Order dated August 26, 2010 under submission.
Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. Court Reporter: Nancy Presant-McDonald, CSR 906, 800-288-3376 (Reported)

OTHER SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-10-2016 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. THOMS J. KLITGAARD, Esq. of Dillingham & Murphy, LLP (Tel No. 415-397-2700) appearing for S.F. Bay Area Council of Boy Scouts of America;
DOUGLAS J. FARMER, JOHN H. RINGGENBERG, and KEN MEHLHORN also present as representatives from S.F. Bay Area Council of Boy Scots of America;
Attys. KEVIN P. DWIGHT, ESQ. and STEPHANIE ROESER, ESQ. of Manatt, Phelps & Phillips, LLP (Tel No. 415-291-7464) appearing for The San Francisco Foundation;
SINDY L. CRAIG and FRED BLACKWELL also present as representatives from The San Francisco Foundation;
Atty. ANDREW ZABRONSKY, ESQ. of EVANS, Latham & Campisi (Tel No. 415-421-0288) appearing for Wells Fargo Bank, N.A.;
JACQUELINE DE ANDA also present as a representative from Wells Fargo Bank, N.A.

The Court conducts an unreported settlement conference with counsel for parties and the representatives for parties. The parties reach a written Stipulation for Settlement which is to be filed by attorney Mr. Zabronsky.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (305/MEW)

Petition For Instructions To Trustee And For Order Modifying Order Dated August 26,2010
Off Calendar Mar-07-2016 Continued To Mar-21-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition Title Assigned Per Direction Of Clerks Office For Filing Purposes And For Purpose Of Computing Fees In Reality And In Substance: Notice Of Application For A Determination Whether Filing And Proceeding With The Attached Petition Would Fall Within The Scope Of Article Vi, Section H Of The Jessie And Carl Wente Charitable Trust( Removal Clause Probate Code Section 21320
Off Calendar Oct-21-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Thomas Klitgaard for the Boy Scouts. Andrew Zabronsky for Wells Fargo Bank. Kevin Dwight for the San Francisco Foundation. Ken Melhorn (Scout Executive) also present. Cindy Craig (SF Foundation) also present.

Mr. Klitgaard stated that he thinks they have amended their petition but can start over, that in August they raised the issue of conflict of interest, release and jurisdiction, that they want this teed up perfect for the court to be able to rule, that the arguments are in two categories, procedural - in regards to notice, and substance - can a safe harbor provision raise conflict of interest, the consequence of a void order, a question of interpretation and that there is no evidence of the intent of the Wente's as to what would happen when the laws changed, that Wells Fargo is sitting on an undistributed amount of money in the amount of $300,000.00 or more, that they do not want to divert principal to someone who was not a primary beneficiary, that Wells Fargo will not distribute the funds until the court rules, that they want the record to reflect that relief has been asked for and that Wells Fargo never responded to their initial filing. Mr. Zabronsky stated that the only issue before the court is the safe harbor issues, that Wells Fargo has made it clear that its position is that it takes no position to the petition filed, that they will remain neutral, that the petition was filed less than a week before responses were due, that injunctive relief is not proper and that if procedural objections are overruled they will ask for leave to file substantive responses. Mr. Dwight stated that they take a neutral position, that their primary goal is to preserve the donors intent, that they would ask the court to review the no contest provision, that the SF Foundation opposes the Boy Scouts request for discovery into the bank accounts, that the Boy Scouts are trying to prohibit distributions to the Foundation and that they ask the court to determine if the Boy Scouts have leave to file their petition. Mr. Klitgaard cited 21320, that they have followed the requirements, asked how Wells Fargo can be a neutral trustee if it has a relationship with the Foundation, why give up the void order issue, that the Foundation was only to come into the picture if the Boy Scouts did not need the money and that all of this is costing the Boy Scouts money. Mr. Zabronsky stated that they have fiduciary and charities here trying to do the right thing, that they have only insisted on getting proper time to file a response, that the request does not have merit and they suggest the Boy Scouts do not pursue it, that they will take no position on the Boy Scouts trying to change the 2010 order, that with the second supplement they seek claims against Wells Fargo with no merit and that they ask the Boy Scouts to consider withdrawing the claims as they have no merit. Mr. Dwight stated that he agrees and joins with the Wells Fargo arguments, that a "handshake" relationship between the Foundation and Wells Fargo does not exist and asks the court to consider solely if the Boy Scouts can file the petition. The Court stated that the parties are to prepare proposed order and exchange them by October 2, 2015, that they are to file any objections to the order and submit proposed orders to the court by October 7, 2015 and the notice of application for a determination whether filing and proceeding with the attached petition would fall within the scope of article VI, etc., is under submission as of that date.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Watts. Court Reporter: Maximillian Alexander Contreras, CSR #13876, 415-987-5746 (Reported)

Petition For Instructions To Trustee Re Distributions And For Order Combining Trusts
Off Calendar Aug-26-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Patricia Connolly for Wells Fargo Bank.

The Court granted the request to merge the Trusts. The Boy Scouts is to get all of the income. Anything above and in excess of the minimum mandatory distribution will go to the San Francisco Foundation. Ms. Connolly is to prepare/submit the order.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.
